pattern: Repeated division by a base maps an integer onto a positional digit string, and two such conversions concatenate cleanly.
hints: Squaring and cubing the input gives two numbers; each must be rendered in its own base.
A base conversion is repeated remainder-and-divide, reading digits from least significant to most, where digit values 10 and above become letters.
Build base-16 of n*n and base-36 of n*n*n by peeling off remainders, mapping 0-9 to digits and 10+ to 'A' onward, reverse each, then join the two strings.

edge_inputs.description: smallest input gives single digits in both bases
base-36 part needs a letter digit
value equal to the second base produces trailing zeros

stress.description: every value in the legal range to catch any digit-mapping slip
stress.note: tiny range so the full sweep is cheap
